evals.prompt: I'm setting up a new Android project. Where should the business logic for calculating order totals live — in the ViewModel, a UseCase, or the Repository?
evals.expected_output: Places business logic in a UseCase in the Domain layer (pure Kotlin, no Android deps). ViewModel orchestrates, Repository handles data access only.

assertions.description: Does not put logic in ViewModel

evals.prompt: My Android app is getting large. How should I split it into modules? I have a login screen, a product listing, and a cart.
evals.expected_output: Recommends feature modules (:feature:login, :feature:products, :feature:cart) plus core modules (:core:network, :core:ui). App module is the DI root and navigation host.

assertions.description: Mentions App module as DI root

evals.prompt: The UI in my Android app is calling the repository directly from a Composable. Is that a problem?
evals.expected_output: Yes — violates Clean Architecture. UI should only access data via ViewModel. Repository belongs in Data layer, not accessible from UI/Composable.

should_not_trigger: How do I hoist state from a Composable up to its parent?
Write a ViewModel that exposes a StateFlow of products
Add an OkHttp interceptor that attaches a Bearer token to all requests
My Composable recomposes too often — how do I fix that?
Set up Hilt with @HiltAndroidApp and inject into my ViewModel
Create a Room DAO for a User entity with insert and query
How do I navigate to a product detail screen using Compose Navigation?
Write a CoroutineWorker that syncs data every 6 hours with WorkManager
My LazyColumn items don't have keys — will that cause issues?
Configure Detekt to fail the build when a method exceeds 30 lines
